column1: Decides if there should be a limit of how many bones can be referenced per vertex. The limit is set by MaxBonePerVertex.
column2: Decides if there should be a limit of how many bones can be referenced per vertex. The limit is set by MaxBonePerVertex.

column1: Decides the maximum limit of how many bones can be connected to each vertex. If for example set to 1, then each vertex will only be linked to 1 bone.
column2: Decides the maximum limit of how many bones can be connected to each vertex. If for example set to 1, then each vertex will only be linked to 1 bone.

column1: Defines the output pixel color space to use. Please note that all internal processing is done in linear space, including super sampling mixing. Conversion to gamma space is done after mixing, right before conversion to the output pixel format.
column2: Defines the output pixel color space to use. Please note that all internal processing is done in linear space, including super sampling mixing. Conversion to gamma space is done after mixing, right before conversion to the output pixel format.

column1: Deprecated: Use NumberOfColumns and NumberOfRows instead. If NumberOfColumns or NumberOfRows are nonzero, they will be used instead. Determines the number of views generated for the flip book impostor. Value has to be a squared number.
column2: Deprecated: Use NumberOfColumns and NumberOfRows instead. If NumberOfColumns or NumberOfRows are nonzero, they will be used instead. Determines the number of views generated for the flip book impostor. Value has to be a squared number.

column1: Determines how many billboards to create. A large BillboardDensity means that more billboards will be created to represent spatially spread out triangles better. Having a low BillboardDensity means that fewer billboards will be created and the distances from original leaves to billboards will get larger. The range is 0->1.
column2: Determines how many billboards to create. A large BillboardDensity means that more billboards will be created to represent spatially spread out triangles better. Having a low BillboardDensity means that fewer billboards will be created and the distances from original leaves to billboards will get larger. The range is 0->1.

column1: Determines the geometric complexity of the billboards. The value range is 0->1, where a value of Zero means that the billboards will be simple and not follow the projection closely. And a value of One means that the geometric complexity will be increased and follow the projection closely.
column2: Determines the geometric complexity of the billboards. The value range is 0->1, where a value of Zero means that the billboards will be simple and not follow the projection closely. And a value of One means that the geometric complexity will be increased and follow the projection closely.

column1: Determines the maximum amount of planes that are computed for the scene initially. When this criteria is met, it is not guaranteed that all triangles in the scene have been accurately mapped to a billboard. It is not a definite hard limit on the number of billboards because the planes can be split up into multiple planes when they´re being cut to match their projections. For instance: if GeometricComplexity is high and there are two completely separated projections on a billboard, then the billboard will be split up into two billboards.
column2: Determines the maximum amount of planes that are computed for the scene initially. When this criteria is met, it is not guaranteed that all triangles in the scene have been accurately mapped to a billboard. It is not a definite hard limit on the number of billboards because the planes can be split up into multiple planes when they´re being cut to match their projections. For instance: if GeometricComplexity is high and there are two completely separated projections on a billboard, then the billboard will be split up into two billboards.

column1: Determines the number of columns in the output atlas. If both NumberOfColumns and NumberOfRows are left at zero, the deprecated NumberOfViews will be used instead.
column2: Determines the number of columns in the output atlas. If both NumberOfColumns and NumberOfRows are left at zero, the deprecated NumberOfViews will be used instead.

column1: Determines the triangle reduction ratio for the trunk part that has been separated from the foliage part based on the ´separate foliage´ settings.
column2: Determines the triangle reduction ratio for the trunk part that has been separated from the foliage part based on the ´separate foliage´ settings.

column1: Determines whether to enable separation between foliage and trunk in a vegetation scene. Use the SeparateFoliage[...] settings to determine how parts are separated.
column2: Determines whether to enable separation between foliage and trunk in a vegetation scene. Use the SeparateFoliage[...] settings to determine how parts are separated.

column1: Determines whether to prioritize accurately mapping triangles with higher visibility to billboards.
column2: Determines whether to prioritize accurately mapping triangles with higher visibility to billboards.

column1: Determines whether to prioritize generating vertical billboards which means they will be optimized to be viewed from the side. Otherwise, if the geometry in the input scene is mostly facing upwards/downwards then the generated billboards will also be facing upwards/downwards which makes them not well suited to being viewed from the side.
column2: Determines whether to prioritize generating vertical billboards which means they will be optimized to be viewed from the side. Otherwise, if the geometry in the input scene is mostly facing upwards/downwards then the generated billboards will also be facing upwards/downwards which makes them not well suited to being viewed from the side.

column1: Determines which input material channel to use for opacity. Some standard material channels are defined as SG_MATERIAL_CHANNEL_[ CHANNEL ] but arbitrary names for user created channels works also.
column2: Determines which input material channel to use for opacity. Some standard material channels are defined as SG_MATERIAL_CHANNEL_[ CHANNEL ] but arbitrary names for user created channels works also.

column1: Determines which method to use when aggregating the UVs. TextureSizeProportions - Aggregated UV charts will be scaled to keep their relative pixel density relative to all other UV charts. The user can still set the size of the output texture maps. SurfaceArea - Aggregated UV charts will have their size set based on its actual geometrical size. OriginalPixelDensity - The combined atlas will be resized to fit each chart so that all charts retain the same amount of pixels as they originally had. This will override any manually set texture size. UVSizeProportions - Aggregated UV charts will have their size set based on its original UV size, disregarding the size of the texture they are used in.
column2: Determines which method to use when aggregating the UVs. TextureSizeProportions - Aggregated UV charts will be scaled to keep their relative pixel density relative to all other UV charts. The user can still set the size of the output texture maps. SurfaceArea - Aggregated UV charts will have their size set based on its actual geometrical size. OriginalPixelDensity - The combined atlas will be resized to fit each chart so that all charts retain the same amount of pixels as they originally had. This will override any manually set texture size. UVSizeProportions - Aggregated UV charts will have their size set based on its original UV size, disregarding the size of the texture they are used in.

column1: Determines which method to use when aggregating the UVs. TextureSizeProportions - Aggregated UV charts will be scaled to keep their relative pixel density relative to all other UV charts. The user can still set the size of the output texture maps. SurfaceArea - Aggregated UV charts will have their size set based on its actual geometrical size. OriginalPixelDensity - The combined atlas will be resized to fit each chart so that all charts retain the same amount of pixels as they originally had. This will override any manually set texture size. UVSizeProportions - Aggregated UV charts will have their size set based on its original UV size, disregarding the size of the texture they are used in.´);.
column2: Determines which method to use when aggregating the UVs. TextureSizeProportions - Aggregated UV charts will be scaled to keep their relative pixel density relative to all other UV charts. The user can still set the size of the output texture maps. SurfaceArea - Aggregated UV charts will have their size set based on its actual geometrical size. OriginalPixelDensity - The combined atlas will be resized to fit each chart so that all charts retain the same amount of pixels as they originally had. This will override any manually set texture size. UVSizeProportions - Aggregated UV charts will have their size set based on its original UV size, disregarding the size of the texture they are used in.´);.

column1: Determines which method to use when generating the texture coordinates. Parameterizer generate from scratch based on the geometry. ChartAggregator generate based on the original texture coordinates.
column2: Determines which method to use when generating the texture coordinates. Parameterizer generate from scratch based on the geometry. ChartAggregator generate based on the original texture coordinates.

column1: Enable bone count as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set bone count has been reached.
column2: Enable bone count as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set bone count has been reached.

column1: Enable bone ratio as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set bone-ratio has been reached.
column2: Enable bone ratio as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set bone-ratio has been reached.

column1: Enable max deviation as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set max deviation has been reached.
column2: Enable max deviation as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set max deviation has been reached.

column1: Enable on screen size as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set on screen size has been reached.
column2: Enable on screen size as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set on screen size has been reached.

column1: Enable Polygon count as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set Polygon count has been reached. Note that a pair of two triangles linked by the QuadFlags field to designate a quad counts as *one* polygon, not two. Also note that using this target will disable reduction for continuous areas of triangles within the quad mesh.
column2: Enable Polygon count as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set Polygon count has been reached. Note that a pair of two triangles linked by the QuadFlags field to designate a quad counts as *one* polygon, not two. Also note that using this target will disable reduction for continuous areas of triangles within the quad mesh.

column1: Enable Polygon ratio as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set Polygon-ratio has been reached. Note that a pair of two triangles linked by the QuadFlags field to designate a quad counts as *one* polygon, not two. Also note that using this target will disable reduction for continuous areas of triangles within the quad mesh.
column2: Enable Polygon ratio as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set Polygon-ratio has been reached. Note that a pair of two triangles linked by the QuadFlags field to designate a quad counts as *one* polygon, not two. Also note that using this target will disable reduction for continuous areas of triangles within the quad mesh.

column1: Enable triangle count as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set triangle count has been reached.
column2: Enable triangle count as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set triangle count has been reached.

column1: Enable triangle ratio as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set triangle-ratio has been reached.
column2: Enable triangle ratio as a reduction target. The reducer will stop when the set triangle-ratio has been reached.

column1: Enables culling object insides and permits use of GeometryCullingSettings. If both this and visibility related culling is enabled, this is run first, then visibility.
column2: Enables culling object insides and permits use of GeometryCullingSettings. If both this and visibility related culling is enabled, this is run first, then visibility.

column1: Enables or disables the bone reduction processing that actually removes bones. Even with this flag off, the bones per vertex can still be limited.
column2: Enables or disables the bone reduction processing that actually removes bones. Even with this flag off, the bones per vertex can still be limited.

column1: Export mode for referenced external files in the scene, such as references to textures. File formats which do not support external references will ignore the setting and always embed the data.
column2: Export mode for referenced external files in the scene, such as references to textures. File formats which do not support external references will ignore the setting and always embed the data.

column1: Flips the winding of the triangles of the output, making frontfaces backfaces and vice versa. Good for conservative z testing.
column2: Flips the winding of the triangles of the output, making frontfaces backfaces and vice versa. Good for conservative z testing.

column1: Generate unique material names if set to true, by replacing the default scheme of appending a sequential material index to the name with appending a GUID. This will make the material names unique each time the pipeline is run, even on the same input scene.
column2: Generate unique material names if set to true, by replacing the default scheme of appending a sequential material index to the name with appending a GUID. This will make the material names unique each time the pipeline is run, even on the same input scene.

column1: HoleFilling defines how aggressively the remesher will fill holes and cavities. What this does is to measure the volume and hole opening sizes of concavities in the input geometry and fill them in in the remeshing if the concavity is large enough with a small enough opening. This removes a lot of unnecessary geometry and is recommended to be kept at it´s default rather than turning it off, it will in the vast majority of cases have a positive effect on the result.
column2: HoleFilling defines how aggressively the remesher will fill holes and cavities. What this does is to measure the volume and hole opening sizes of concavities in the input geometry and fill them in in the remeshing if the concavity is large enough with a small enough opening. This removes a lot of unnecessary geometry and is recommended to be kept at it´s default rather than turning it off, it will in the vast majority of cases have a positive effect on the result.

column1: How far away a surface has to be from another surface to generate no occlusion. Only applicable if SimpleOcclusionMode is off.
column2: How far away a surface has to be from another surface to generate no occlusion. Only applicable if SimpleOcclusionMode is off.

column1: If a chart contains triangles with different windings, for instance if the chart is mirrored, then split up the chart at the folding crease. The setting is only used if SeparateOverlappingCharts is enabled.
column2: If a chart contains triangles with different windings, for instance if the chart is mirrored, then split up the chart at the folding crease. The setting is only used if SeparateOverlappingCharts is enabled.

column1: If a group of non-visible triangles (connected to visible triangles) has an area below the FillNonVisibleAreaThreshold - it will receive the same visibility as the neighboring visible triangles. Set to zero to skip filling nonvisible regions.
column2: If a group of non-visible triangles (connected to visible triangles) has an area below the FillNonVisibleAreaThreshold - it will receive the same visibility as the neighboring visible triangles. Set to zero to skip filling nonvisible regions.

column1: If automatic_texture_size is enabled, then force the texture sizes to be a power of 2.
column2: If automatic_texture_size is enabled, then force the texture sizes to be a power of 2.

column1: If converting vertex colors to weights, this setting along with WeightsFromColorMultiplier controls how to map the RGBA intensity between 0-1 to weights. Standard mode maps color intensity [0-1] to vertex weight [1/Multiplier - Multiplier]. High mode maps color intensity [0-1] to vertex weight [1 - Multiplier]. Low mode maps color intensity [0-1] to vertex weight [1/Multiplier - 1].
column2: If converting vertex colors to weights, this setting along with WeightsFromColorMultiplier controls how to map the RGBA intensity between 0-1 to weights. Standard mode maps color intensity [0-1] to vertex weight [1/Multiplier - Multiplier]. High mode maps color intensity [0-1] to vertex weight [1 - Multiplier]. Low mode maps color intensity [0-1] to vertex weight [1/Multiplier - 1].

column1: If converting vertex colors to weights, this setting controls the max and min resulting vertex weights generated by the input color.
column2: If converting vertex colors to weights, this setting controls the max and min resulting vertex weights generated by the input color.

column1: If converting vertex colors to weights, this setting controls which color component is used to create the weights.
column2: If converting vertex colors to weights, this setting controls which color component is used to create the weights.

column1: If enabled import file information is passed to the scene exporter for merging the contents of the import file into the resulting exported file.(Only used by FBXExporter).
column2: If enabled import file information is passed to the scene exporter for merging the contents of the import file into the resulting exported file.(Only used by FBXExporter).

column1: If enabled the cascaded scenes are flattened in depth first order. Name clashes are removed by prefixing with _LOD[Index]. Where index is index of the scene after flattening. (Only valid for FBX files)
column2: If enabled the cascaded scenes are flattened in depth first order. Name clashes are removed by prefixing with _LOD[Index]. Where index is index of the scene after flattening. (Only valid for FBX files)

column1: If enabled will create a MappingImage per view, instead of concatenating them all into the same atlas. Fetch the indexed MappingImages using GetMappingImageForImageIndex() on the ImpostorProcessor.
column2: If enabled will create a MappingImage per view, instead of concatenating them all into the same atlas. Fetch the indexed MappingImages using GetMappingImageForImageIndex() on the ImpostorProcessor.

column1: If enabled, connected parts will not be projected onto separate billboards. For example if the input asset has large leaf geometries that consist of many connected triangles, then MaintainLeafConnections will ensure that all the connected triangles are projected to the same billboard.
column2: If enabled, connected parts will not be projected onto separate billboards. For example if the input asset has large leaf geometries that consist of many connected triangles, then MaintainLeafConnections will ensure that all the connected triangles are projected to the same billboard.

column1: If enabled, will aggregate all the valid original UVs. If there are invalid original UVs, those parts will receive new parameterized UVs.
column2: If enabled, will aggregate all the valid original UVs. If there are invalid original UVs, those parts will receive new parameterized UVs.

column1: If enabled, will compute the visibility conservatively - meaning that triangles that are visible will be tagged as visible but some non-visible triangles might also be tagged as visible. If this is turned off, then it is no longer guaranteed that all visible triangles are found - but more non-visible triangles will be identified as non-visible.
column2: If enabled, will compute the visibility conservatively - meaning that triangles that are visible will be tagged as visible but some non-visible triangles might also be tagged as visible. If this is turned off, then it is no longer guaranteed that all visible triangles are found - but more non-visible triangles will be identified as non-visible.

column1: If enabled, will remove all the visible triangles that are not occluding any other triangle. It will also remove all non-visible triangles.
column2: If enabled, will remove all the visible triangles that are not occluding any other triangle. It will also remove all non-visible triangles.

column1: If false, the processor will remove all geometry and scene meshes which were not selected for processing from the scene. If true, only the geometry in the ProcessSelectionSet will be replaced or updated, while the rest of the scene tree is kept intact. New geometry is always added as the last child of the scene root. If generating a mappingimage to cast materials, new materialIds will also be appended to the end of the old material table instead of replacing the old ones.
column2: If false, the processor will remove all geometry and scene meshes which were not selected for processing from the scene. If true, only the geometry in the ProcessSelectionSet will be replaced or updated, while the rest of the scene tree is kept intact. New geometry is always added as the last child of the scene root. If generating a mappingimage to cast materials, new materialIds will also be appended to the end of the old material table instead of replacing the old ones.

column1: If false, the processor will remove all geometry and scene nodes which was not selected for processing from the scene. If true, only the geometry in the ProcessSelectionSet will be replaced or updated. If old nodes are kept and a new geometry is added to the scene, it will always be the last child of the scene root. If generating a mappingimage to cast materials, new materialIds will also be appended to the end of the old material table instead of replacing the old ones.
column2: If false, the processor will remove all geometry and scene nodes which was not selected for processing from the scene. If true, only the geometry in the ProcessSelectionSet will be replaced or updated. If old nodes are kept and a new geometry is added to the scene, it will always be the last child of the scene root. If generating a mappingimage to cast materials, new materialIds will also be appended to the end of the old material table instead of replacing the old ones.

column1: If FlipBackfacingNormals is on, this decides which source normal we should compare the destination normal with when deciding if the normal is back-facing or not. Changing this to FaceNormal can be beneficial for two-sided assets like leaves that can get weirdly flipped pixel normals with the default setting. When using the FaceNormal option together with transparency the front-most layer with an opacity of above 50% will be used.
column2: If FlipBackfacingNormals is on, this decides which source normal we should compare the destination normal with when deciding if the normal is back-facing or not. Changing this to FaceNormal can be beneficial for two-sided assets like leaves that can get weirdly flipped pixel normals with the default setting. When using the FaceNormal option together with transparency the front-most layer with an opacity of above 50% will be used.

column1: If multiple UV charts overlap and have the same material, SurfaceAreaScale specifies which of those instances determines the the output scale. It is only valid when the ChartAggregatorMode is SurfaceArea and SeparateOverlappingCharts is false.
column2: If multiple UV charts overlap and have the same material, SurfaceAreaScale specifies which of those instances determines the the output scale. It is only valid when the ChartAggregatorMode is SurfaceArea and SeparateOverlappingCharts is false.

column1: If OverrideFlipbookTextureWidth is set to a nonzero value it will determine the total width of the texture atlas. The height will be determined by the ratio of NumberOfRows to the NumberOfColumns. If OverrideFlipbookTextureWidth is not set the width and height will be set per flipbook view using SetTextureWidth() in the MappingImageSettings.
column2: If OverrideFlipbookTextureWidth is set to a nonzero value it will determine the total width of the texture atlas. The height will be determined by the ratio of NumberOfRows to the NumberOfColumns. If OverrideFlipbookTextureWidth is not set the width and height will be set per flipbook view using SetTextureWidth() in the MappingImageSettings.

column1: If PivotAroundOrigin is enabled, the Flipbook quad geometry will be centered around the origin instead of the object center to match the pivoted rotation of the original geometry. This creates less optimal texture usage however, and the further the object center is from the origin the larger the texture waste becomes (in the direction away from the object center). Usage example: if the subject is a tree with an off-center trunk base, then flipping between the views will have the base of the trunk moving around. But if the trunk is centered on the origin and PivotAroundOrigin is enabled, the base of the trunk will remain stationary.
column2: If PivotAroundOrigin is enabled, the Flipbook quad geometry will be centered around the origin instead of the object center to match the pivoted rotation of the original geometry. This creates less optimal texture usage however, and the further the object center is from the origin the larger the texture waste becomes (in the direction away from the object center). Usage example: if the subject is a tree with an off-center trunk base, then flipping between the views will have the base of the trunk moving around. But if the trunk is centered on the origin and PivotAroundOrigin is enabled, the base of the trunk will remain stationary.

column1: If set to true, geometry that is not visible will be removed.
column2: If set to true, geometry that is not visible will be removed.

column1: If set to true, the displacement data will be output directly to tessellated attributes in the scene geometries, rather than to an image. This setting requires that the Scene object is set in the caster.
column2: If set to true, the displacement data will be output directly to tessellated attributes in the scene geometries, rather than to an image. This setting requires that the Scene object is set in the caster.

column1: If set to true, the opacity data will be output directly to tessellated attributes in the scene geometries, rather than to an image. This setting requires that the Scene object is set in the caster.
column2: If set to true, the opacity data will be output directly to tessellated attributes in the scene geometries, rather than to an image. This setting requires that the Scene object is set in the caster.

column1: If set to true, the size of the displacement vector components in the direction of the interpolated normal are stored in the displacement map, instead of the displacement vectors.
column2: If set to true, the size of the displacement vector components in the direction of the interpolated normal are stored in the displacement map, instead of the displacement vectors.

column1: If set to true, visibility weights will be used in the reducer.
column2: If set to true, visibility weights will be used in the reducer.

column1: If set to true, visibility weights will be used when generating new texture coordinates.
column2: If set to true, visibility weights will be used when generating new texture coordinates.

column1: If set, all original texcoord sets are replaced by a single new one which will be used for the mapping image. This means the output scene will be temporarily invalid before the materialtable has been updated to reflect the new changes. This flag has no effect on the remeshing, which always does a full retexturing.
column2: If set, all original texcoord sets are replaced by a single new one which will be used for the mapping image. This means the output scene will be temporarily invalid before the materialtable has been updated to reflect the new changes. This flag has no effect on the remeshing, which always does a full retexturing.

column1: If set, and generating new texcoords using either the parameterizeror the chart aggregator, the relative sizes of the output charts will be scaled using the weights in the ´VertexWeights´ vertex field.
column2: If set, and generating new texcoords using either the parameterizeror the chart aggregator, the relative sizes of the output charts will be scaled using the weights in the ´VertexWeights´ vertex field.

column1: If set, and using the ReductionProcessor or QuadReductionProcessor, the reduction will be weighted based on the weights in the ´VertexWeights´ vertex field.
column2: If set, and using the ReductionProcessor or QuadReductionProcessor, the reduction will be weighted based on the weights in the ´VertexWeights´ vertex field.

column1: If set, cameras from the input scene will be used to define what angles the occlusion mesh can be visible from, and optimize the output mesh accordingly. Otherwise, the occlusion mesh will be assumed to be viewable from all directions.
column2: If set, cameras from the input scene will be used to define what angles the occlusion mesh can be visible from, and optimize the output mesh accordingly. Otherwise, the occlusion mesh will be assumed to be viewable from all directions.

column1: If set, it will generate new normals for the Geometry, and if not set, it will keep the normals from the original normal set (or not add normals at all if none were present). Please note that if the original geometry contains normals, the normal repairer will replace invalid normals even if ReplaceNormals is set to false.
column2: If set, it will generate new normals for the Geometry, and if not set, it will keep the normals from the original normal set (or not add normals at all if none were present). Please note that if the original geometry contains normals, the normal repairer will replace invalid normals even if ReplaceNormals is set to false.

column1: If set, it will generate new tangents and bitangents for the Geometry, and if not set, it will keep the new tangents and bitangents from the original normal set (or not add tangents and bitangents at all if none were present).
column2: If set, it will generate new tangents and bitangents for the Geometry, and if not set, it will keep the new tangents and bitangents from the original normal set (or not add tangents and bitangents at all if none were present).

column1: If set, normals that are invalid will be replaced. All others are left intact. Bad normals are those that are either zero length, or simply points away from the surface.
column2: If set, normals that are invalid will be replaced. All others are left intact. Bad normals are those that are either zero length, or simply points away from the surface.

column1: If set, objects containing locked vertices will not be removed.
column2: If set, objects containing locked vertices will not be removed.

column1: If set, objects with small surface area but big diameter will also be removed.
column2: If set, objects with small surface area but big diameter will also be removed.

column1: If set, occlusion will not scale with distance, each ray will only be either fully occluded or not at all. Speeds up processing time.
column2: If set, occlusion will not scale with distance, each ray will only be either fully occluded or not at all. Speeds up processing time.

column1: If set, only vertices that are on the border (as reported by the VertexBorder boolean field) are considered for the welding.
column2: If set, only vertices that are on the border (as reported by the VertexBorder boolean field) are considered for the welding.

column1: If set, only vertices that are within the same material are welded. Vertices on material borders are locked.
column2: If set, only vertices that are within the same material are welded. Vertices on material borders are locked.

column1: If set, only vertices that are within the same scene node are welded.
column2: If set, only vertices that are within the same scene node are welded.

column1: If set, only vertices that belong to different scene nodes are considered for the welding. Affects both welder and t-junction remover.
column2: If set, only vertices that belong to different scene nodes are considered for the welding. Affects both welder and t-junction remover.

column1: If set, output textures are exported in sRGB format. Input texture format is determined by the flag in the corresponding texture node in the material definition.
column2: If set, output textures are exported in sRGB format. Input texture format is determined by the flag in the corresponding texture node in the material definition.

column1: If set, perfectly flat shaded surfaces will be created, where possible. Normals adjacent to perfectly geometrically flat surfaces will be recalculated to have the same direction as for those of the adjacent flat surface.
column2: If set, perfectly flat shaded surfaces will be created, where possible. Normals adjacent to perfectly geometrically flat surfaces will be recalculated to have the same direction as for those of the adjacent flat surface.

column1: If set, t-junctions will be removed. If enabled, it also requires UseWelding to be on.
column2: If set, t-junctions will be removed. If enabled, it also requires UseWelding to be on.

column1: If set, the aggregation processor, reduction processor, or remeshing processor will create a mapping image that maps triangles on the reduced geometry back to triangles on the original geometry. The texture is insert into the MappingImage object. The image data will contain two fields: * RidArray, ´TriangleIds´, TupleSize:1, The id of the triangle  * UnsignedShortArray, ´BarycentricCoords´, TupleSize:2, Items A & B of the barycentric coordinate on the triangle. The user can control the size of the mapped texture using the values MappingTextureWidth and MappingTextureHeight.
column2: If set, the aggregation processor, reduction processor, or remeshing processor will create a mapping image that maps triangles on the reduced geometry back to triangles on the original geometry. The texture is insert into the MappingImage object. The image data will contain two fields: * RidArray, ´TriangleIds´, TupleSize:1, The id of the triangle  * UnsignedShortArray, ´BarycentricCoords´, TupleSize:2, Items A & B of the barycentric coordinate on the triangle. The user can control the size of the mapped texture using the values MappingTextureWidth and MappingTextureHeight.

column1: If set, the displacement vectors are transformed into the destination object tangent space.
column2: If set, the displacement vectors are transformed into the destination object tangent space.

column1: If set, the geometries defined in the matching selection set will be used to clip the process geometry. What side of the geometry that will be culled is determined by the triangle facing, so make sure your winding is correct and that triangles point ´up´ toward the space you want to keep. Inconsistent triangle winding or meshes that do not have a clear local inside and outside will cause inconsistent results when used as clipping geometries.
column2: If set, the geometries defined in the matching selection set will be used to clip the process geometry. What side of the geometry that will be culled is determined by the triangle facing, so make sure your winding is correct and that triangles point ´up´ toward the space you want to keep. Inconsistent triangle winding or meshes that do not have a clear local inside and outside will cause inconsistent results when used as clipping geometries.

column1: If set, the green channel is assumed to point along the negative bitangent instead of the positive. This is roughly equivalent to switching between left- and righthanded, but some rounding may differ.
column2: If set, the green channel is assumed to point along the negative bitangent instead of the positive. This is roughly equivalent to switching between left- and righthanded, but some rounding may differ.

column1: If set, the impostor will contain both front and back facing surfaces.

column1: If set, the impostor will exactly conform to the bounds of the geometry. If false, its dimensions will always be diameter*diameter.
column2: If set, the impostor will exactly conform to the bounds of the geometry. If false, its dimensions will always be diameter*diameter.

column1: If set, the influence of a triangle normal upon the vertex normals is scaled by the angle of the corner at the vertex.

column1: If set, the normal caster calculates the bitangent used for encoding per-fragment using the normal, tangent and bitangent sign instead of interpolating the actual bitangent vertex data. This needs to match the behavior of the renderer to not produce rendering artifacts.
column2: If set, the normal caster calculates the bitangent used for encoding per-fragment using the normal, tangent and bitangent sign instead of interpolating the actual bitangent vertex data. This needs to match the behavior of the renderer to not produce rendering artifacts.

column2: If set, the normal caster normalizes the normal and tangent data used for encoding after interpolation. This needs to match the behavior of the renderer to not produce rendering artifacts.

column1: If set, the sampling hemispheres will be flipped to align with the normals of the destination geometry rather than with the source geometry. This can be used to get more consistent sampling directions when using remeshing of input geometries with inconsistently aligned normals.
column2: If set, the sampling hemispheres will be flipped to align with the normals of the destination geometry rather than with the source geometry. This can be used to get more consistent sampling directions when using remeshing of input geometries with inconsistently aligned normals.

column1: If set, the tangentspaces already existing in the scene will be readjusted so that the tangent and binormal are both orthogonal to the normal. Will be needed since most of the time the normals are changed in the reduction process.
column2: If set, the tangentspaces already existing in the scene will be readjusted so that the tangent and binormal are both orthogonal to the normal. Will be needed since most of the time the normals are changed in the reduction process.

column1: If set, the total opacity of the baked layers replaces the alpha value in the output texture.
column2: If set, the total opacity of the baked layers replaces the alpha value in the output texture.

column1: If set, the vertex normals channel will be sampled from the original mesh.
column2: If set, the vertex normals channel will be sampled from the original mesh.

column1: If set, then normals will be flipped if they are back facing, i.e. pointing into the surface. This may introduce artifacts on geometries that have correctly facing normals, so only use for geometries with known back-facing normals.
column2: If set, then normals will be flipped if they are back facing, i.e. pointing into the surface. This may introduce artifacts on geometries that have correctly facing normals, so only use for geometries with known back-facing normals.

column1: If set, then the generated normal map will be in tangent space. Please note that the geometry casted to must contain tangent space fields.
column2: If set, then the generated normal map will be in tangent space. Please note that the geometry casted to must contain tangent space fields.

column1: If set, vertex color channels will be sampled from the original mesh.
column2: If set, vertex color channels will be sampled from the original mesh.

column2: If set, vertices occupying the same position in 3D space will be welded together, creating better connectivity for quad reduction.

column1: If set, vertices within each others welding distance will be welded together.
column2: If set, vertices within each others welding distance will be welded together.

column1: If set, will cast a multi-layer mapping image front-to-back, rather than the default back-to-front.
column2: If set, will cast a multi-layer mapping image front-to-back, rather than the default back-to-front.

column2: If set, will lock the geometric border of the geometry, and not reduce at all. This setting might be useful if the geometry needs to fit a neighbor geometry, but will reduce overall reduction quality (geometric quality over reduction ratio)

column1: If the flag is set, the normal calculation will only allow hard edges along texture borders. Any vertex normal within a texture chart (not along the border) will be forced soft. column2: If the flag is set, the normal calculation will only allow hard edges along texture borders. Any vertex normal within a texture chart (not along the border) will be forced soft. Caveat: If this is enabled, some normals might be highly interpolated, and deviate substantially from the triangle normal.

column1: If the index of an existing vertex color field in the input geometry is set here, the existing VertexWeights field will be overwritten by a set created by converting the selected color field according to the other WeightsFromColor settings in this settings object. If both this and the corresponding ColorName is set, the processors will prioritize the name.
column2: If the index of an existing vertex color field in the input geometry is set here, the existing VertexWeights field will be overwritten by a set created by converting the selected color field according to the other WeightsFromColor settings in this settings object. If both this and the corresponding ColorName is set, the processors will prioritize the name.

column1: If the name of an existing vertex color field in the input geometry is set here, the existing VertexWeights field will be overwritten by a set created by converting the selected color field according to the other WeightsFromColor settings in this settings object. If both ColorName and the corresponding ColorLevel is set, the processors will prioritize the name.
column2: If the name of an existing vertex color field in the input geometry is set here, the existing VertexWeights field will be overwritten by a set created by converting the selected color field according to the other WeightsFromColor settings in this settings object. If both ColorName and the corresponding ColorLevel is set, the processors will prioritize the name.

column1: If this flag is set, the caster will only run if there is at least one input material that has the main input channel which is to be cast. If the flag is not set, the caster will always produce an output, even if it is totally empty/unset.
column2: If this flag is set, the caster will only run if there is at least one input material that has the main input channel which is to be cast. If the flag is not set, the caster will always produce an output, even if it is totally empty/unset.

column1: If true, and also generating one or more mapping images for material casting, the material IDs in the process geometry will be updated to reflect the new, not yet cast output materials. This means the output scene will be temporarily invalid before the materialtable has been updated to reflect the new changes.
column2: If true, and also generating one or more mapping images for material casting, the material IDs in the process geometry will be updated to reflect the new, not yet cast output materials. This means the output scene will be temporarily invalid before the materialtable has been updated to reflect the new changes.

column1: If true, generates a new tangent space fields at position specified by TexCoordLevel, based on the vertex normals and texture coordinates. Tangent space fields will always be created if missing, and GenerateMappingImage is enabled. If GenerateTexCoords is enabled, new tangents will always be generated.
column2: If true, generates a new tangent space fields at position specified by TexCoordLevel, based on the vertex normals and texture coordinates. Tangent space fields will always be created if missing, and GenerateMappingImage is enabled. If GenerateTexCoords is enabled, new tangents will always be generated.

column2: If true, generates a new texcoords field at the position specified by TexCoordLevel. Will be ignored and always generate texcoords if none are available in the Geometry if GenerateMappingImage is enabled.

column1: If true, the QuadFlags triangle field in the input geometries (used for marking triangle pairs as quads for use in the QuadReductionProcessor) will be maintained in the processing geometries through the triangle reduction process as well as possible. This will cause most polygons involved in collapses performed by the triangle reducer to become triangles. This allows sequential triangle->quad reductions to handle mixed geometries. Setting this to false removes the QuadFlags triangle field entirely, ensuring the output geometry is 100% triangles with no implicit quads.
column2: If true, the QuadFlags triangle field in the input geometries (used for marking triangle pairs as quads for use in the QuadReductionProcessor) will be maintained in the processing geometries through the triangle reduction process as well as possible. This will cause most polygons involved in collapses performed by the triangle reducer to become triangles. This allows sequential triangle->quad reductions to handle mixed geometries. Setting this to false removes the QuadFlags triangle field entirely, ensuring the output geometry is 100% triangles with no implicit quads.

column1: If true, the remeshing processor will allow mapped transparency in the generated mapping image. This allows material casting where gaps filled by the remeshing processor will be cast as transparent.
column2: If true, the remeshing processor will allow mapped transparency in the generated mapping image. This allows material casting where gaps filled by the remeshing processor will be cast as transparent.

column1: If UseOverrideCenterAndRadius is enabled, the OverrideCenter and OverrideRadius determine the center and size of the impostor.
column2: If UseOverrideCenterAndRadius is enabled, the OverrideCenter and OverrideRadius determine the center and size of the impostor.

column2: If UseOverrideCenterAndRadius is enabled, the OverrideRadius value determines the size of the impostor. If OverrideRadius is equal to 0 then the radius will be automatically calculated internally to contain the entire scene.

column1: Is used to separate the trunk from the foliage in a vegetation scene by comparing each group of connected triangles in the scene with the scene´s largest group. If a group´s triangle count is below SeparateFoliageTriangleRatio * largestGrouptriangleCount it is treated as foliage and processed into billboards. The trunk is optimized using triangle reduction. The range is 0.0->1.0. 0.0 means nothing will be foliage, 1.0 means everything will be foliage.
column2: Is used to separate the trunk from the foliage in a vegetation scene by comparing each group of connected triangles in the scene with the scene´s largest group. If a group´s triangle count is below SeparateFoliageTriangleRatio * largestGrouptriangleCount it is treated as foliage and processed into billboards. The trunk is optimized using triangle reduction. The range is 0.0->1.0. 0.0 means nothing will be foliage, 1.0 means everything will be foliage.

column1: Is used to separate the trunk from the foliage in a vegetation scene by explicitly specifying the foliage material names as a JSON array of strings. Example: [´fMat0´, ´fMat1´]. If the value is not a JSON array, the value is parsed as the name of a single foliage material.
column2: Is used to separate the trunk from the foliage in a vegetation scene by explicitly specifying the foliage material names as a JSON array of strings. Example: [´fMat0´, ´fMat1´]. If the value is not a JSON array, the value is parsed as the name of a single foliage material.

column1: Is used to separate the trunk from the foliage in a vegetation scene by finding each group of connected triangles in the scene and if the diameter of the group is below (SeparateFoliageSizeThreshold * SceneDiameter) it is treated as foliage and processed into billboards. The trunk is optimized using reduction. The range is 0->large value. 0 means nothing will be foliage, value larger than the scene´s diameter means everything will be foliage.
column2: Is used to separate the trunk from the foliage in a vegetation scene by finding each group of connected triangles in the scene and if the diameter of the group is below (SeparateFoliageSizeThreshold * SceneDiameter) it is treated as foliage and processed into billboards. The trunk is optimized using reduction. The range is 0->large value. 0 means nothing will be foliage, value larger than the scene´s diameter means everything will be foliage.

column1: Is used to separate the trunk from the foliage in a vegetation scene by finding each group of connected triangles in the scene and if the surface area is below (SeparateFoliageAreaThreshold * AreaOfTheLargestGroupInTheScene) it is treated as foliage and processed into billboards. The trunk is optimized using reduction. The range is 0->large value. 0 means nothing will be foliage, value larger than the maximum surface area means everything will be foliage.
column2: Is used to separate the trunk from the foliage in a vegetation scene by finding each group of connected triangles in the scene and if the surface area is below (SeparateFoliageAreaThreshold * AreaOfTheLargestGroupInTheScene) it is treated as foliage and processed into billboards. The trunk is optimized using reduction. The range is 0->large value. 0 means nothing will be foliage, value larger than the maximum surface area means everything will be foliage.

column1: Is used to separate the trunk from the foliage in a vegetation scene by finding each group of connected triangles in the scene and if the triangle count is below SeparateFoliageTriangleThreshold it is treated as foliage and processed into billboards. The trunk is optimized using reduction. The range is 0->large value. 0 means nothing will be foliage, value larger than the maximum triangle count means everything will be foliage.
column2: Is used to separate the trunk from the foliage in a vegetation scene by finding each group of connected triangles in the scene and if the triangle count is below SeparateFoliageTriangleThreshold it is treated as foliage and processed into billboards. The trunk is optimized using reduction. The range is 0->large value. 0 means nothing will be foliage, value larger than the maximum triangle count means everything will be foliage.

column1: Limits the allowed size of the triangles in the output mesh, allowing generation of more uniform triangle distribution and tesselation across flat planes. The unit of this limit is the same as the currently active RemeshingTarget, i.e. it is in pixels on-screen when using OnScreenSize, and in absolute scene units when using MaxDeviation. Setting this value to 0 disables the feature.
column2: Limits the allowed size of the triangles in the output mesh, allowing generation of more uniform triangle distribution and tesselation across flat planes. The unit of this limit is the same as the currently active RemeshingTarget, i.e. it is in pixels on-screen when using OnScreenSize, and in absolute scene units when using MaxDeviation. Setting this value to 0 disables the feature.

column1: Normally, the processor will generate an impostor with texcoords from 0.0 to 1.0, giving perfect UV coverage in the final texture. If your engine uses permanent tiling rendering mode, you may need to add a small padding to avoid sub-pixel wrapping artifact at the impostor edges, which this setting supplies. Ex: Setting this to 0.01 will generate texcoords in the range 0.01 to 0.99.
column2: Normally, the processor will generate an impostor with texcoords from 0.0 to 1.0, giving perfect UV coverage in the final texture. If your engine uses permanent tiling rendering mode, you may need to add a small padding to avoid sub-pixel wrapping artifact at the impostor edges, which this setting supplies. Ex: Setting this to 0.01 will generate texcoords in the range 0.01 to 0.99.

column2: Only applicable if BillboardMode: Foliage. Determines if the scene is intended to be viewed from both sides without back face culling.

column1: Set flag to control if this pipeline step is an intermediate step where the processed scene is only used as input to the next cascaded pipeline and not for export or further access. This will discard any processed data once all cascaded pipelines are done, and the scene will be ignored during export. Useful if you want to group two pipelines together for one output, for example an aggregation and a reduction pipeline where you only want the output from the combined aggregation + reduction.
column2: Set flag to control if this pipeline step is an intermediate step where the processed scene is only used as input to the next cascaded pipeline and not for export or further access. This will discard any processed data once all cascaded pipelines are done, and the scene will be ignored during export. column1: Set the reduction performance mode. column1: Set the relative geometrical accuracy of the output mesh. A value less than 1 will lower the accuracy, and lower the output triangle count, but might introduce intersections in the output geometry. A value above 1 will introduce more triangles, and will be able to capture the input geometry more accurately in the output.
column2: Set the relative geometrical accuracy of the output mesh. A value less than 1 will lower the accuracy, and lower the output triangle count, but might introduce intersections in the output geometry. A value above 1 will introduce more triangles, and will be able to capture the input geometry more accurately in the output.

column1: Set the topological accuracy of the output mesh. 1=default accuracy, 0=minimal topological accuracy. Lowering the accuracy will lower the memory requirements, and increase remeshing performance, at the cost of lower topological accuracy, where the remesher fills in larger gaps or holes.
column2: Set the topological accuracy of the output mesh. 1=default accuracy, 0=minimal topological accuracy. Lowering the accuracy will lower the memory requirements, and increase remeshing performance, at the cost of lower topological accuracy, where the remesher fills in larger gaps or holes.

column1: Set to true and the reducer will do a much more precise way of calculating the vertex normals. It will however be slower. Set to false and it will do a much more rough estimation of what the vertex normals will look like, but faster.

column2: The camera selection set name. This determines the selection set used for the cameras for when UseCameras is on. If set to null, all cameras in the scene will be used. If both selection set name and ID are set, the name will be used.

column1: The color space edge threshold, which will define how many hard color borders there will be. Corners more similar than the threshold will be merged. This works similarly to HardEdgeAngle for normals; lower values leads more hard borders between neighboring corner colors, and higher values will give you more shared, smoother, colors. This also impacts data usage when using packed geometry since (more unique colors) == (more vertices). The distance is measured in RGBA space.
column2: The color space edge threshold, which will define how many hard color borders there will be. Corners more similar than the threshold will be merged. This works similarly to HardEdgeAngle for normals; lower values leads more hard borders between neighboring corner colors, and higher values will give you more shared, smoother, colors. This also impacts data usage when using packed geometry since (more unique colors) == (more vertices). The distance is measured in RGBA space.

column1: The CreateGeomorphGeometry value. column2: The CreateGeomorphGeometry value. column1: The fill mode determines what to do with the pixels that remain unfilled after both the casting and dilation has been performed. Options are:  NoFill = Do not fill remaining pixels  Interpolate = linearly interpolate the closest samples  NearestNeighbor = use the closest available pixel value without interpolation.
column2: The fill mode determines what to do with the pixels that remain unfilled after both the casting and dilation has been performed. Options are:  NoFill = Do not fill remaining pixels  Interpolate = linearly interpolate the closest samples  NearestNeighbor = use the closest available pixel value without interpolation.

column1: The flag that decides how precise the reducer will be during the mesh reduction. Fast -> Reducer will be faster, but onscreensize and trianglecount will not match perfectly.  Consistent -> Reducer will be slower, but onscreensize and trianglecount will consistently give matching results.
column2: The flag that decides how precise the reducer will be during the mesh reduction. Fast -> Reducer will be faster, but onscreensize and trianglecount will not match perfectly.  Consistent -> Reducer will be slower, but onscreensize and trianglecount will consistently give matching results.

column1: The flag that specifies how big ´freedom´ Simplygon has when it comes to altering vertex/triangle data. Allowing more altered data means it will be easier to create good looking LODs, but without altered data it will be easier to keep the memory-usage in the LOD-chain more optimized. Note: When using OnlyUseOriginalData, the geometry being reduced can not have a node transformation matrix other than the identity matrix.
column2: The flag that specifies how big ´freedom´ Simplygon has when it comes to altering vertex/triangle data. Allowing more altered data means it will be easier to create good looking LODs, but without altered data it will be easier to keep the memory-usage in the LOD-chain more optimized. Note: When using OnlyUseOriginalData, the geometry being reduced can not have a node transformation matrix other than the identity matrix.

column1: The GeometryDataFieldIndex setting, which specifies what field index to cast in the GeometryDataCaster, for fields where it is applicable (Coords, TexCoords, Normals, Tangents, Bitangents and Colors, TriangleId and MaterialIds). The setting is ignored for other field types.
column2: The GeometryDataFieldIndex setting, which specifies what field index to cast in the GeometryDataCaster, for fields where it is applicable (Coords, TexCoords, Normals, Tangents, Bitangents and Colors, TriangleId and MaterialIds). The setting is ignored for other field types.

column1: The GeometryDataFieldType setting, which specifies what field type to cast in the GeometryDataCaster. The VertexId field, user field and custom field types are not supported. They are however supported in the ComputeCaster.
column2: The GeometryDataFieldType setting, which specifies what field type to cast in the GeometryDataCaster. The VertexId field, user field and custom field types are not supported. They are however supported in the ComputeCaster.

column1: The height of the texture of the output mapping image.
column2: The height of the texture of the output mapping image.

column1: The importance value of the bone-weights of the vertices. The more impact a vertex has on the skinning of the Geometry (for example, vertices close to joints), the more likely it is to be kept. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0
column2: The importance value of the bone-weights of the vertices. The more impact a vertex has on the skinning of the Geometry (for example, vertices close to joints), the more likely it is to be kept. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0

column1: The importance value of the boundary between triangles from different sub-geometries. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0
column2: The importance value of the boundary between triangles from different sub-geometries. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0

column1: The importance value of the boundary between triangles with different materials. For example, if triangles with a metallic material are connected to triangles with a wood material, this setting will determine how important it is to keep the border between them intact. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0
column2: The importance value of the boundary between triangles with different materials. For example, if triangles with a metallic material are connected to triangles with a wood material, this setting will determine how important it is to keep the border between them intact. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0

column1: The importance value of the position of the vertices and triangles of the Geometry, or the silhouette of the Geometry. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0
column2: The importance value of the position of the vertices and triangles of the Geometry, or the silhouette of the Geometry. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0

column1: The importance value of the UV coordinates of the Geometry, both the boundary of the UV charts, as well as the UV stretch across the triangles. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0
column2: The importance value of the UV coordinates of the Geometry, both the boundary of the UV charts, as well as the UV stretch across the triangles. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0

column1: The importance value of the vertex-colors of the Geometry, both the boundary between triangles with different colors, as well as how the colors interpolate over the surface of the triangles. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0
column2: The importance value of the vertex-colors of the Geometry, both the boundary between triangles with different colors, as well as how the colors interpolate over the surface of the triangles. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0

column1: The importance value of the vertex-normals of the Geometry, both the sharp edges, as well as how the normals interpolate over the surface of the triangles. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0
column2: The importance value of the vertex-normals of the Geometry, both the sharp edges, as well as how the normals interpolate over the surface of the triangles. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0

column1: The importance value of user selected edges. Can be used when a Boolean UserCornerField with the name SgEdgeSelectionSet is added to the Geometry. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0
column2: The importance value of user selected edges. Can be used when a Boolean UserCornerField with the name SgEdgeSelectionSet is added to the Geometry. The importance is relative to the values of the other mesh features (or disabling an importance entirely by setting it to 0). A higher number means higher importance. Default is 1.0

column1: The inferior/minimum value of field values to map onto the image data range (0->1). The minimum value will be represented by 0 in the image. Note that the mapping is only used for real value fields, not id-based fields, which cannot be scaled accurately.
column2: The inferior/minimum value of field values to map onto the image data range (0->1). The minimum value will be represented by 0 in the image. Note that the mapping is only used for real value fields, not id-based fields, which cannot be scaled accurately.

column1: The inward move multiplier, how much the mesh is allowed to shrink its silhouette perimeter.
column2: The inward move multiplier, how much the mesh is allowed to shrink its silhouette perimeter.

column1: The KeepSymmetry flag. If set, the reducer will detect symmetric features, and retain symmetry during processing. Please note that for a geometry to be considered symmetrical, also the texture coordinates must be mirrored.
column2: The KeepSymmetry flag. If set, the reducer will detect symmetric features, and retain symmetry during processing. Please note that for a geometry to be considered symmetrical, also the texture coordinates must be mirrored.

column1: The LimitMaxEdgeLength flag. If set, the reducer will limit the maximum edge length of the triangles after reduction to the size defined by MaxEdgeLength. Triangles that are already larger than this before reduction are unaffected.
column2: The LimitMaxEdgeLength flag. If set, the reducer will limit the maximum edge length of the triangles after reduction to the size defined by MaxEdgeLength. Triangles that are already larger than this before reduction are unaffected.

column1: The LimitMaxEdgeLength flag. If set, the remesher will limit the maximum edge length of the generated triangles to the size specified in MaxEdgeLength.
column2: The LimitMaxEdgeLength flag. If set, the remesher will limit the maximum edge length of the generated triangles to the size specified in MaxEdgeLength.

column1: The MappingLayerIndex setting, which specifies the layer of the mapping image to use for mapping the geometry data. This is useful eg if you have decals which are on layer 0, and the real geometry on layer 1
column2: The MappingLayerIndex setting, which specifies the layer of the mapping image to use for mapping the geometry data. This is useful eg if you have decals which are on layer 0, and the real geometry on layer 1

column1: The material mapping for the input material this setting object corresponds to, meaning what material of the generated LOD the corresponding input material will be baked into. Both InputMaterialCount and OutputMaterialCount need to be set for this mapping to work, and all original materials need to be mapped to an existing output id.
column2: The material mapping for the input material this setting object corresponds to, meaning what material of the generated LOD the corresponding input material will be baked into. Both InputMaterialCount and OutputMaterialCount need to be set for this mapping to work, and all original materials need to be mapped to an existing output id.

column1: The MaxDeviation value, the maximum surface-deviation between the reduced geometry and the original.
column2: The MaxDeviation value, the maximum surface-deviation between the reduced geometry and the original.

column1: The maximum length of the triangles edges after reduction. Can be used to limit output triangle size. The size is defined in absolute scene units.
column2: The maximum length of the triangles edges after reduction. Can be used to limit output triangle size. The size is defined in absolute scene units.

column1: The maximum number of layers in the mapping image. If a remeshed geometry has a lot of transparent triangles, use a higher number to be able to find intersections on the inside of parts of the geometry. This setting only has effect in the remeshing, when running reduction the number of layers is always 1.
column2: The maximum number of layers in the mapping image. If a remeshed geometry has a lot of transparent triangles, use a higher number to be able to find intersections on the inside of parts of the geometry. This setting only has effect in the remeshing, when running reduction the number of layers is always 1.

column1: The minimum number of pixels between charts of the output mapping image.
column2: The minimum number of pixels between charts of the output mapping image.

column1: The multi-sampling level of the output mapping image. Values 1-8 are accepted.
column2: The multi-sampling level of the output mapping image. Values 1-8 are accepted.

column1: The name of the input material channel to cast. Existing user channels of any arbitrary name in the input materials can be cast.
column2: The name of the input material channel to cast. Existing user channels of any arbitrary name in the input materials can be cast.

column1: The name of the input opacity channel to use for combining layers in most casters and casting opacity in the opacitycaster. User channels of any arbitrary name can be used. If the caster is run through a Pipeline object, the OpacityChannel set here will also be set as the output materials OpacityChannel property.
column2: The name of the input opacity channel to use for combining layers in most casters and casting opacity in the opacitycaster. User channels of any arbitrary name can be used. If the caster is run through a Pipeline object, the OpacityChannel set here will also be set as the output materials OpacityChannel property.

column1: The name of the output material. If the output material count is greater than one, an index separated with an underscore will be appended to the material names, such that MyMaterial becomes MyMaterial_0, MyMaterial_1 and so on.
column2: The name of the output material. If the output material count is greater than one, an index separated with an underscore will be appended to the material names, such that MyMaterial becomes MyMaterial_0, MyMaterial_1 and so on.

column1: The name of the selection set containing all the scene cameras and scene meshes used when computing visibility. If scene meshes are added, all their vertices will be used as omni-directional cameras. If set to null, all cameras in the scene will be used. If both selection set name and ID are set, the name will be used.
column2: The name of the selection set containing all the scene cameras and scene meshes used when computing visibility. If scene meshes are added, all their vertices will be used as omni-directional cameras. If set to null, all cameras in the scene will be used. If both selection set name and ID are set, the name will be used.

column1: The name of the selection set containing all the scene meshes used when computing visibility. If set to null, no occluders in the scene will be used. If both selection set name and ID are set, the name will be used.
column2: The name of the selection set containing all the scene meshes used when computing visibility. If set to null, no occluders in the scene will be used. If both selection set name and ID are set, the name will be used.

column1: The name of the selection set that contains all of the bones that will be forced to be removed in the BoneLOD process, UNLESS they are locked. If a bone is set to be removed, all its descendants will be removed also. If both name and id are set for the remove selection set, the name will be used.
column2: The name of the selection set that contains all of the bones that will be forced to be removed in the BoneLOD process, UNLESS they are locked. If a bone is set to be removed, all its descendants will be removed also. If both name and id are set for the remove selection set, the name will be used.

column1: The name of the selection set that contains all of the bones that will be locked, and cannot be removed in the BoneLOD process. If a bone is locked, all its ancestors will be locked also. If both name and id are set for the locked selection set, the name will be used.
column2: The name of the selection set that contains all of the bones that will be locked, and cannot be removed in the BoneLOD process. If a bone is locked, all its ancestors will be locked also. If both name and id are set for the locked selection set, the name will be used.

column1: The normal hard angle cutoff in degrees.
column2: The normal hard angle cutoff in degrees.

column1: The number of progressive passes. Minimum is 1, but higher numbers give a better quality, at the expense of longer running time.
column2: The number of progressive passes. Minimum is 1, but higher numbers give a better quality, at the expense of longer running time.

column1: The occlusion mode. This decides if the processor generates an occluder (which is smaller than the original mesh), an occludee (which is larger), or an output that just tries to match the input.
column2: The occlusion mode. This decides if the processor generates an occluder (which is smaller than the original mesh), an occludee (which is larger), or an output that just tries to match the input.

column1: The onscreen size (px) of the output utility mesh. This will determine triangle count and quality.
column2: The onscreen size (px) of the output utility mesh. This will determine triangle count and quality.

column1: The OnScreenErrorTolerance determines how large of an error that is tolerated, in pixels. Valid range is 1-50. Since this setting is based around the absolute worst-case scenario, you can usually get good results at relatively high tolerances.
column2: The OnScreenErrorTolerance determines how large of an error that is tolerated, in pixels. Valid range is 1-50. Since this setting is based around the absolute worst-case scenario, you can usually get good results at relatively high tolerances.

column1: The OnScreenSize value, the maximum surface-deviation between the reduced geometry and the original.
column2: The OnScreenSize value, the maximum surface-deviation between the reduced geometry and the original.

column1: The OnScreenSize value, used to calculate the maximum surface-deviation allowed, by assuming errors less than 1 pixel onscreen will not be noticeable.
column2: The OnScreenSize value, used to calculate the maximum surface-deviation allowed, by assuming errors less than 1 pixel onscreen will not be noticeable.

column1: The opacity type determines how the opacity channel of this material should be saved. To make (0.0->1.0) map to (transparent-opaque), use ´Opacity´. To make (0.0->1.0) map to (opaque-transparent), use ´Transparency´
column2: The opacity type determines how the opacity channel of this material should be saved. To make (0.0->1.0) map to (transparent-opaque), use ´Opacity´. To make (0.0->1.0) map to (opaque-transparent), use ´Transparency´

column1: The outward move multiplier, how much the mesh is allowed to grow its silhouette perimeter.
column2: The outward move multiplier, how much the mesh is allowed to grow its silhouette perimeter.

column2: The path for the batch tool used when executing pipelines in a new/distributed process. This should be the path to the directory where the SimplygonBatch executable is located. If the tool is in the system path this setting should be an empty string.

column1: The path where the pipeline will save any created textures from material casters. A relative path will be treated as relative to the scene file output path if the pipeline is executed with scene file paths (RunSceneFromFile), or from the current working directory if the pipeline is executed with a scene object (RunScene). The directory must exist, it will not be created.
column2: The path where the pipeline will save any created textures from material casters. A relative path will be treated as relative to the scene file output path if the pipeline is executed with scene file paths (RunSceneFromFile), or from the current working directory if the pipeline is executed with a scene object (RunScene). The directory must exist, it will not be created.
